input_text:
Decided where to store feature flags.

The database approach (storing flags in a database table) is attractive because you can change flags without deploying. But it means your application has a runtime dependency on a database query to decide whether a feature is enabled. If the database is slow or unreachable, feature flag reads can fail. It also creates a hidden config state that's not visible in code review.

Environment variables: simple, explicit, visible in your deployment config, no runtime dependency. To change a flag, you change an env var and redeploy. For our deployment velocity (3-4 deploys per day), this is fast enough.

Going with environment variables.

expected.text: Store feature flags in environment variables, not a database table — avoids runtime DB dependency and hidden config state; deploy velocity (3-4/day) makes redeploy-to-flip acceptable

reviewer_notes: Architectural choice for where dynamic configuration lives.
